name: NIP-61
description: Nutzaps: Receiving—and verifying— zaps, has always been a weak point in nostr. Nutzaps have enabled a new, more straightforward and verifiable way to participate in the zap-economy.

name: Nostr Safebox
description: Nostr Safebox is the concept of a ‘safebox’ for Nostr. It’s a generalization of the wallet concept for storing secure, private personal information.The idea is that the safebox contains different item types, wallets, records, etc. You can give a client access by providing the nsec of the safebox. If you no longer trust the client, you can transfer the data to another safebox and delete the current one (no guarantees on deletion, of course)

name: Nsite

name: Tollgate
description: Freedom-enhancing internet infrastructure with four core modules: Merchant (e-cash payments), Valve (captive portal access control), Crows Nest (gateway discovery), and Janitor (updates via NIP-94). Successfully deployed at multiple events and now focusing on automated router-to-router payments and improved mobile clients. Demonstrates that free markets with small, frequent payments using bearer assets are the most efficient way to connect devices.

name: Nostr-based Tollgate

Uses captive portals and MAC address tracking to allow anonymous internet access while still enabling proper accounting of purchased access.

description: An MDX (Markdown with JSX) tokenizer and parser written in Zig

name: Zig Nostr Loader
description: A WASM-first Nostr event loader built with Zig, designed for high-performance caching and deduplication

name: nihao
description: Nostr identity setup and health-check CLI. Creates a complete Nostr identity (keypair, profile, relay list, DM relays, lightning address, Cashu wallet) in one command. Audits existing npub health with a 0–8 score. Single Go binary, non-interactive, agent-friendly. Also available as an OpenClaw skill.

name: One Npub
description: Single npub backed by FROST threshold signatures. Distributes key shares in a 2-of-3 multisig style for nostr identities. If one share is compromised, rotate shares while keeping the npub safe.

name: Nomen
description: Persistent, searchable memory for AI agents using nostr as the sync layer. WoT-integrated with five visibility tiers from public to agent-private, hybrid search combining semantic vectors and full-text, and sleep-inspired consolidation. Works as MCP server, Context-VM, or embedded Rust library.

name: Fleetgner
description: Delegated nostr signer that runs as an MCP server. Granular access controls where admin grants specific users permission to sign with specific keys, with optional policies constraining what can be signed. NIP-46 support for existing clients plus CVM/MCP tools for agents.

name: Nostr Signal
description: Discover and rank trusted local businesses using nostr reputation signals. Combines activity mentions, endorsements, and zaps with customisable weights. Client-side posting via nostr browser extensions, no server needed.

name: Chatstr
description: Sane defaults for nostr. Apps pull defaults from your nostr activity or your web of trust for a better, customized experience with fewer footguns.

name: Decentralized DNS via Namecoin
description: Flutter app for censorship-resistant, human-readable nostr identities via Namecoin. Resolves names like alice.bit to nostr pubkeys stored on-chain, replacing NIP-05 which relies on censorable web servers. Supports NIP-17 encrypted DMs.

name: OAuth Bunker
description: Bridges OAuth providers (Google, GitHub, Microsoft, Apple) with nostr identity via NIP-46 remote signing. Split architecture keeps nsecs in an isolated LAN-only bunker. Admin panel protected by NIP-98 HTTP auth with scoped signing permissions.

name: Silent Payments via NIP-05
description: Send bitcoin to human-readable nostr identities instead of 118-char silent payment addresses. Resolves NIP-05 names to BIP 352 addresses via nostr profile lookup with Schnorr signature verification. Implemented as a resolver for Sparrow Wallet.

name: FriendPub
description: Secure key rotation for nostr using threshold cryptography and NIP-17 encrypted DMs. 2-of-3 guardian threshold signature scheme where at least two trusted guardians must approve a rotation. Publishes rotation proofs publicly for network verification.

name: Portal SDK
description: Identity and payment layer for bitcoin-native applications. Passwordless auth via nostr, one-time and recurring Lightning payments, NWC-compatible outbound payments, and non-monetary Cashu tokens for access control. No accounts, no KYC, no payment processor.

name: Pika
description: Chat via MLS (Messaging Layer Security) over nostr. End-to-end encrypted group messaging with forward secrecy. Includes an OpenClaw plugin for agent integration.

name: CashuTube
description: Video streaming platform with Cashu ecash micropayments. Spilman payment channels for pay-per-view without accounts or subscriptions. HLS adaptive bitrate streaming with Cashu wallet integration.
